updateMessage: Adds experimental United States 2026 federal tax rules, employee FICA, and U.S. brokerage, Roth, and RSU wrappers.

incomeTax.name: Federal Income Tax
incomeTax.tooltip: U.S. federal ordinary-income tax for 2026, with the filing-status standard deduction embedded as a 0% bracket. Itemized deductions, most credits, Social Security benefit taxation, and other targeted deductions are documented in the gap report.

regionalIncomeTax.name: State Income Tax
regionalIncomeTax.tooltip: Optional state-level income tax profile for representative jurisdictions. Select a state profile through country inputs to apply state tax on top of federal tax.

socialContributions.name: Social Security Tax
socialContributions.tooltip: Employee OASDI payroll tax at 6.2% up to the 2026 wage base of USD 184,500. Self-employment tax, railroad taxes, and employer-side payroll costs are not modeled.

socialContributions.name: Medicare Tax
socialContributions.tooltip: Employee Medicare payroll tax at 1.45% with no wage cap. The separate 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax is modeled below as a distinct levy using a simplified threshold approximation.

additionalTaxes.name: Additional Medicare Tax
additionalTaxes.tooltip: 0.9% federal Additional Medicare Tax on high employment income. FinSim uses the USD 200,000 threshold as a simple proxy; real thresholds vary by filing status and are documented in the gap report.

capitalGainsTax.name: Capital Gains Tax
capitalGainsTax.tooltip: Simplified federal long-term capital-gains proxy using a flat 15% rate and no annual exemption. The real U.S. system depends on filing status, taxable-income thresholds, holding period, and NIIT, all of which are documented in the gap report.

helpText.label: Tax-Deferred Retirement
helpText.contributionDescriptionP1:
Enter how much of the modeled 2026 employee elective-deferral limit you want to contribute from salary into a tax-deferred retirement account.

FinSim includes age-based catch-up cap adders (50+ and ages 60-63) and keeps employer-match formulas and plan-specific eligibility out of scope.
helpText.contributionDescriptionP2:
Enter how much of your partner's modeled 2026 employee elective-deferral limit they want to contribute from salary into a tax-deferred retirement account.

FinSim applies the same U.S. retirement-account cap and age-based catch-up logic for both people.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP1:
Enter your expected monthly Social Security retirement benefit.

Use your latest SSA statement or retirement estimate. The simulator does not model the real provisional-income formula that can make part of Social Security taxable.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP2:
Enter your partner's expected monthly Social Security retirement benefit.

Use their latest SSA statement or retirement estimate. The simulator does not model the real provisional-income formula that can make part of Social Security taxable.

investmentTypes.label: Taxable Brokerage
investmentTypes.helpText: Approximation of U.S. taxable brokerage holdings such as listed shares, mutual funds, and ETFs. Sale gains use the simplified federal long-term capital-gains rate of ${taxRules.capitalGainsTax.rate,percentage}; dividends, NIIT, and short-term ordinary-income treatment are not modeled.

investmentTypes.label: Roth IRA
investmentTypes.helpText: Roth IRA growth and qualified withdrawals are modeled as tax-free. FinSim does not enforce contribution limits, income-based eligibility, ordering rules, or the five-year / age tests for qualified distributions.

investmentTypes.label: RSUs
investmentTypes.helpText: Restricted Stock Units are modeled as ordinary income when they vest and are automatically sold. Later sale gains are approximated at ${taxRules.capitalGainsTax.rate,percentage}; payroll-tax withholding and exact RSU basis mechanics are documented in the gap report.
